sourceMovie.title: Lost in Starlight
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Lost in Starlight (2025) as a animation theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The animation and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Upside Down
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Lost in Starlight clicked for you, Upside Down (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in romance and science fiction, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Lost in Starlight?
faq.answer: Try Upside Down, Starman, WALL·E—each shares DNA with Lost in Starlight while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Lost in Starlight?
faq.answer: Lost in Starlight (2025) is best described as Animation, Romance, Science Fiction, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Lost in Starlight worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this animation lane.
